intro: The clearest difference between Snyk and StackHawk is pricing — Snyk is hybrid with a free tier, while StackHawk is subscription (no free tier). Beyond that, lock-in — Snyk has low lock-in (Low-Medium — CLI + IDE integration, standard vulnerability reports), whereas StackHawk has low lock-in (Based on OSS ZAP scanner). Snyk fits Developer-first security scanning in CI/CD; StackHawk fits marketplace, apps. On the strengths side, Snyk is cited for Developer-first security scanning in CI/CD, while StackHawk is cited for UGC platforms (social, marketplace). The honest trade-off: Snyk is the wrong call for budget projects; StackHawk is the wrong call for internal projects; Snyk users flag just need basic dependency audit — npm audit is free, and StackHawk users flag B2B products without user content. Choosing between them should come down to the constraint that actually binds your workflow, not surface-level overlap. On developer experience the data shows Snyk at 5/5 and StackHawk at 5/5, with transparency at 3/5 and 4/5 respectively.
quick_take: Snyk is for Developer-first security scanning in CI/CD; StackHawk is for marketplace, apps; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Snyk if your use case is Developer-first security scanning in CI/CD, and a hybrid with a free tier pricing shape fits your budget better than subscription (no free tier).
verdict_b: Choose StackHawk if your use case is marketplace, apps, and a subscription (no free tier) pricing shape fits your budget better than hybrid with a free tier.

faq.a: Snyk is hybrid with a free tier, while StackHawk is subscription, so the cheaper option depends on your usage shape. Listed pricing pages usually change, so check both directly before committing. For small teams, the free-tier limits often matter more than headline prices.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Snyk to StackHawk?
faq.a: Migrating from Snyk to StackHawk is feasible when their scopes overlap, but the work is in re-mapping configuration, data models, and integrations. Snyk has low lock-in, so exporting state is typically straightforward. Plan for a dual-run period so you can validate parity before decommissioning.

Snyk and StackHawk score the same on developer experience (5/5 each) in our data. Choose based on which workflow, docs style, and CLI ergonomics feel closer to your team's preferences. Trial both in a small project before committing.

faq.q: Is StackHawk a good alternative to Snyk?
faq.a: StackHawk is a reasonable alternative to Snyk when StackHawk fits marketplace, apps. If your use case matches Snyk's sweet spot — Developer-first security scanning in CI/CD — staying with Snyk is probably safer. Snyk itself is not meant for budget projects, which is often where users start shopping for alternatives.
